scenarios.opening_query: My business partner and I haven't spoken in a month and the company is running on autopilot.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Business and Corporate > General (contracts, entities) || Business and Corporate > Litigation
scenarios.hidden_fact: He's locked me out of the bank accounts and hired his own litigator — a member-oppression suit looks unavoidable now.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: He locked me out of the accounts and hired a litigator — a member-oppression suit looks unavoidable.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the deadlock has become adversarial litigation
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's cold silence, not war — we both want a clean buyout negotiated and papered.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's silence, not war — we both want a clean buyout papered.
scenarios.vetting_note: Lockout-plus-counsel is the litigation trigger; row 33's split posture.

scenarios.opening_query: A former employee took our client list to a competitor and our biggest accounts are getting calls.
scenarios.final: Intellectual Property > Computer Law & Internet Law
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Business and Corporate > Litigation || Intellectual Property > Computer Law & Internet Law
scenarios.hidden_fact: Forensics shows he logged into our CRM for weeks AFTER leaving using a shared password — unauthorized computer access is the sharpest claim we have.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Forensics shows he kept logging into our CRM for weeks after leaving — unauthorized access is the sharpest claim.
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No intrusion — he memorized relationships and violated his non-solicit; it's a straight covenant case.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No intrusion — he's violating his non-solicit from memory. Covenant case.
scenarios.vetting_note: Intrusion-evidence fact adds the computer-law route.

scenarios.opening_query: I want to turn my sole proprietorship into something more protected before signing a big contract.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Business and Corporate > General (contracts, entities) || Business and Corporate > Agriculture/Farm
scenarios.hidden_fact: The business is my 200-acre orchard and the contract is a multi-year packing-house supply deal — farm-entity structuring with water rights and FSA loans in the mix.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's my 200-acre orchard, and the contract is a multi-year packing-house deal — farm structuring with water rights and FSA loans involved.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether agricultural assets and programs shape the entity work
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's my web-design studio and the client is a hospital system — a standard LLC plus contract review.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's my web-design studio and a hospital client — standard LLC and contract review.
scenarios.vetting_note: Row 140's farm-vs-generic routing on the formation side.

scenarios.opening_query: Our family restaurant got a violation notice that could close us and we don't understand our options.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Business and Corporate > Restaurants/OLCC || Administrative Law > General (Local/Municipal)
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's the county health department over our outdoor kitchen setup — no liquor issue at all; it's a local administrative appeal.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's the county health department about our outdoor kitchen — nothing to do with liquor. Local administrative appeal.
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's the OLCC — a minor-decoy sting, and our liquor license is what's on the line.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's the OLCC after a minor-decoy sting — the liquor license is on the line.
scenarios.vetting_note: Same restaurant panic; regulator identity routes.

scenarios.opening_query: I invested in my friend's company and now he won't show me the books or return my calls.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Business and Corporate > Litigation || Business and Corporate > Securities
scenarios.hidden_fact: He raised from eleven of us with a slide deck promising 'guaranteed 20% returns' — no disclosures, no filings; a securities-fraud posture, and the state has an investor complaint process.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: He raised from eleven people on a deck promising 'guaranteed 20%' — no disclosures or filings. That's securities fraud territory.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the raise was an unregistered securities offering
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's just me — a documented loan-plus-equity deal between two people, and I want my member rights and repayment enforced.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Just me — a documented loan-plus-equity deal, and I want it enforced.
scenarios.vetting_note: Multi-investor promised-return facts trigger the securities route; single-deal stays contract litigation.

scenarios.opening_query: We're a tiny nonprofit and a lawyer letter just arrived that scared the entire board.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Business and Corporate > Non-Profit || Intellectual Property > Trademark/Copyright
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's a trademark cease-and-desist — a national charity claims our 20-year-old name infringes their newer registration, and the response deadline is two weeks.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's a trademark cease-and-desist — a national charity says our 20-year-old name infringes their newer mark. Two weeks to respond.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the threat is trademark enforcement
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The letter questions our governance — a donor's counsel alleging misuse of restricted funds, which is squarely a nonprofit-compliance matter.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's a donor's lawyer alleging misuse of restricted funds — nonprofit governance and compliance.
scenarios.vetting_note: Letter-subject disclosure; senior-user trademark defense vs charity compliance.
